stories: Good evening Sean, it's Tuesday evening, the fifth of May, 2026
A fact worth noting: Carl Linnaeus — the man who gave every living thing its two-part Latin name — was born on this date in 1707
Three hundred and nineteen years on, his binomial system still names every species on Earth
Nine brings a pre-read review block, then at ten, Big Rock time focused on your FY26 GBSO full-year performance review
Midday has the Daily Update Call on BHP SAP purchase order conversions, then L5 AIP work through to half-one
In Singapore today, parliament passed the Cross-Border Railways Bill, enabling co-location of Customs, Immigration and Quarantine facilities at the upcoming Johor Bahru to Singapore RTS rail stations
The four-kilometre link remains on track to open by end of 2026
New Zealand's Prime Minister Luxon was also in town to sign a trade pact

Good morning Sean, it's Tuesday, the fifth of May, 2026
Today is Cinco de Mayo - the day Mexico's underdog army pulled off one of history's great upsets against Napoleon's French forces at Puebla in 1862
The twist this year: Cinco de Mayo falls on a Tuesday, making it officially Taco Tuesday
Somewhere, a marketing team is very pleased
Six-thirty opens with a Voice of Customer presentation on Teams, then Potash and GBS connect at eight
The Weekly Integrations PwC meeting is at nine on Teams, followed by a Connect call at nine-thirty
Ten brings the L3 MOP, and ten-thirty the FBP Support Model Review
Block eleven for GBSLT Quarterly pre-work

Good evening Sean, it's Monday evening, the fourth of May, 2026
President has ever died in the month of May
Every other month has claimed at least one
May is also the only month you can't abbreviate
